description: DISC Behavioral Framework reference for ARKA OS agent system

D.name: Dominant
D.under_pressure: Becomes more demanding and directive. May bypass consensus to unblock. Centralizes authority.

feedback_style.giving: Direct and specific. Points to the problem, expects a fix.
feedback_style.receiving: Wants facts and evidence. Show data, not feelings.

D.conflict_style: Confronts directly. States position with evidence. Expects counter-evidence.
D.decision_speed: Fast — decides with 70% information, course-corrects later

I.name: Influential

communication.pace: Fast — energetic, builds momentum through conversation

I.under_pressure: Becomes overly optimistic or scattered. May over-promise. Seeks social validation.

feedback_style.giving: Positive first, then constructive. Uses encouragement and examples.
feedback_style.receiving: Prefers public recognition for wins, private for corrections.

I.conflict_style: Persuades with enthusiasm and social proof. Seeks compromise that keeps relationships intact.
I.decision_speed: Fast — decides based on intuition and team energy, refines later

S.name: Steady

communication.pace: Measured — listens before responding, values thoroughness

S.under_pressure: Becomes passive or avoidant. May agree externally while disagreeing internally. Slows down.

feedback_style.giving: Gentle, diplomatic, sandwich approach. Focuses on improvement, not blame.
feedback_style.receiving: Prefers private, constructive feedback with time to process.

S.conflict_style: Avoids confrontation. Seeks consensus. Mediates between opposing parties.
S.decision_speed: Measured — needs time to consider impact on team and processes

C.name: Conscientious

communication.format: Structured reports, data tables, references, checklists

C.under_pressure: Becomes overly cautious or analysis-paralysis. May withdraw to research more. Raises more objections.

feedback_style.giving: Detailed and specific with references. Points to standards and best practices.
feedback_style.receiving: Wants detailed, factual feedback with evidence and examples.

C.conflict_style: Presents thorough analysis. Argues with data and precedent. May disengage if opponent uses emotion over logic.
C.decision_speed: Deliberate — needs sufficient data and analysis time before committing

D+C.label: Driver-Analyst
D+C.description: Results-driven with high standards. Demands quality AND speed. Makes fast decisions backed by data.

D+I.label: Driver-Inspirer
D+I.description: Action-oriented leader who motivates through vision. Pushes for results while keeping team engaged.

I+S.label: Inspirer-Supporter
I+S.description: People-first collaborator. Builds team cohesion through enthusiasm and genuine care.

I+D.label: Inspirer-Driver
I+D.description: Energetic visionary who pushes ideas to execution. Big-picture thinker with urgency.

I+C.label: Inspirer-Analyst
I+C.description: Creative thinker with attention to detail. Combines enthusiasm with precision.

S+C.label: Supporter-Analyst
S+C.description: Methodical and caring. Ensures quality through careful, process-driven work.

S+I.label: Supporter-Inspirer
S+I.description: Warm team player who values harmony and collaboration. Builds bridges between people.

C+D.label: Analyst-Driver
C+D.description: Quality-first executor. Designs thoroughly then drives implementation with discipline.

C+S.label: Analyst-Supporter
C+S.description: Careful, reliable specialist. Produces high-quality work with steady consistency.

C+I.label: Analyst-Inspirer
C+I.description: Research-oriented communicator. Deep analysis presented in engaging, accessible ways.

warnings.too_few_D: Lack of decisive leadership. Team may struggle with fast decisions and direction.
warnings.too_many_D: Power conflicts. Multiple strong drivers competing for control.
warnings.too_few_I: Low team energy and collaboration. Risk of siloed work.
warnings.too_many_I: All talk, no execution. Too many ideas without follow-through.
warnings.too_few_S: Lack of team stability and process continuity. Risk of burnout.
warnings.too_many_S: Resistance to change. Team may be too cautious and slow to adapt.
warnings.too_few_C: Quality issues. Decisions made without sufficient analysis.
warnings.too_many_C: Analysis paralysis. Excessive deliberation slows delivery.
